title: jacquemontia lasioclados
intro: Jacquemontia lasioclados, also known as the 'Sea Beach Morning Glory', is a flowering vine native to tropical Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as treating fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as being used to reduce swelling and pain. In herbal preparations, Jacquemontia lasioclados is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
benefits: Jacquemontia lasioclados has many benefits, such as its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression due to its high levels of flavonoids and terpenoids. This plant has also been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation in the body, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, Jacquemontia lasioclados has been found to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, potentially reducing the risk of certain diseases such as cancer and heart disease. By incorporating Jacquemontia lasioclados into one's lifestyle, individuals may experience improved overall health and well-being, leading to a better quality of life and increased energy levels.
actions: Jacquemontia lasioclados has many therapeutic actions, such as reducing inflammation, which can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with conditions like arthritis, making it easier for people to perform daily tasks like cooking and cleaning. Its antioxidant properties can also help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, thereby reducing the risk of chronic diseases like cancer and heart disease, allowing individuals to maintain their physical activity levels and enjoy hobbies like hiking and dancing. intro_study: According to "Bioorganic chemistry", jacquemontia lasioclados benefits from its extracts, particularly the floral extract and the compound isoorientin (3), which have shown strong antioxidant and cytotoxic effects, indicating potential application in pharmaceutical industries.
constituents: Jacquemontia lasioclados has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, glycosides, terpenoids, alkaloids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which possess various medicinal properties. These constituents, including quercetin, kaempferol, and apigenin, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, thereby helping to alleviate conditions like arthritis, improve heart health, and prevent infections. Additionally, the plant's saponins and glycosides have been shown to possess anti-cancer properties, while its terpenoids and flavonoids may help to reduce blood pressure and cholesterol levels. By harnessing the medicinal potential of Jacquemontia lasioclados, people can benefit from improved cardiovascular health, reduced cancer risk, and enhanced overall well-being.
